title: Review the current kernel/vote/fugue launcher + installer setup for residual correctness risks after recent parity work. Focus on host-neutral paths, shell wrapper compatibility, and remote/mac mini failure modes. Return findings only, prioritized.
summary_text: 1. `HIGH` Remote install defaults are not host-neutral and can target the wrong remote home path. [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:9`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:9), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:133`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:133), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-kernel-chat-plugin.sh:8`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-kernel-chat-plugin.sh:8), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-kernel-chat-plugin.sh:102`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-kernel-chat-plugin.sh:102) 2. `HIGH` “Install successful” does not imply runnable `kernel`/`vote`: installers copy wrappers but do not verify required guard/bridge/prompt prerequisites, while launcher fails closed by default. [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:95`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:95), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:123`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:123), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:252`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:252), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:1057`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:1057) Observed behavior on clean temp HOME: `kernel prompt requires codex-kernel-guard: guard executable or source not found`. 3. `MEDIUM-HIGH` Remote install is partial-state prone: files are streamed/copied before remote `.zshrc` mutation; if final remote `python3` step fails, deployment is half-applied without rollback. [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:123`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:123), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:130`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:130), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-kernel-chat-plugin.sh:96`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-kernel-chat-plugin.sh:96) 4. `MEDIUM` Shell wrapper compatibility is zsh-only (`.zshrc` + zsh function syntax including `/kernel` style names); non-zsh shells do not get equivalent command surface. [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:63`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:63), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:113`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/scripts/install-orchestrator-shell-surface.sh:113), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/assets/orchestrator-shell.zsh:22`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/assets/orchestrator-shell.zsh:22) 5. `MEDIUM` `fugue` launcher root discovery is path-assumption heavy; remote/mac mini checkouts outside the hardcoded candidates fail unless `FUGUE_ROOT` is set. [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/fugue:32`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/fugue:32), [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/fugue:54`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/fugue:54) 6. `MEDIUM` Current host has legacy hardcoded launcher functions in `.zshrc` before the managed block; if managed source fails to load, behavior falls back to machine-specific paths and older seman...
